name: Auto Money
description_short: Auto Money provides vehicle title loans in South Carolina and title pawns in Georgia, offering up to $20,000 same-day cash using your car title as collateral — no credit check required.
description_long:
Auto Money is a consumer title lending company headquartered at 450 Meeting St, Charleston, South Carolina, with 80+ physical store locations across South Carolina and Georgia. The company is not affiliated with any bank or credit union and holds no CDFI, HUD, or NFCC certifications. It is not BBB accredited, and multiple individual store locations carry a "Not Rated" BBB status due to insufficient information or unresolved complaints. Auto Money operates solely as a private title lender, structured to serve customers outside the traditional banking system.

The company's core product is the car title loan in South Carolina, where a customer's lien-free vehicle title serves as collateral for a short-term loan of up to $20,000. In Georgia, the product is structured as a title pawn under state pawn laws. Auto Money also accepts lien-free motorcycle titles and offers to refinance or pay off existing title loans from competing lenders, sometimes at a lower interest rate. Under South Carolina law, monthly interest rates legally range from 1% to 25% — meaning the effective APR can reach up to 300%. Loan terms are typically 30 days. Specific fee schedules and tiered pricing are not publicly disclosed on the company's website.

What distinguishes Auto Money from bank-based lenders is its accessibility: there is no credit check, no bank account required, and approved borrowers can receive cash in under an hour. Customers retain possession of and continue driving their vehicle throughout the loan term. The company's Rock Hill, SC location holds a 5.0/5 Google rating from 171 reviews, suggesting strong in-person customer service at individual branches. Their willingness to pay off competitor title loans positions them as a refinancing option within the title lending market itself.

The most significant limitation of Auto Money is cost: at up to 25% monthly interest, borrowers who carry a balance for a full year face extraordinary total repayment amounts. In Georgia, there is no interest refund if a title pawn is paid off early. The company is in-person only — applications cannot be approved or funded online — restricting access to customers near one of their 80+ SC or GA locations. The lack of BBB accreditation and at least one documented failure to respond to a BBB complaint are legitimate transparency concerns. Auto Money is genuinely useful for vehicle-owning borrowers in financial emergencies with no credit access, but the high cost of capital demands careful consideration before borrowing.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee. Georgia title pawns explicitly do not refund interest on early payoff. No guarantee policy identified.

pros: No credit check required — approval based on vehicle value, not credit history
No bank account required to receive funds
Same-day cash in under 1 hour if approved
Borrower keeps and drives their vehicle throughout the loan term
Loans up to $20,000 depending on vehicle value
Will pay off existing title loans from competing lenders, potentially at a lower rate
80+ store locations across South Carolina and Georgia
cons: Interest rates up to 25% per month (up to 300% APR) under South Carolina law
In-person only — no online application approval or funding; must visit a physical store
Georgia title pawns do not refund interest if paid off early
Not BBB accredited; at least one location documented as failing to respond to a BBB complaint
Limited to South Carolina and Georgia residents near a physical branch location
best_for: Vehicle owners in SC or GA who need fast cash and have poor or no credit history
Borrowers who have been denied by traditional banks or credit unions
People who own a lien-free car or motorcycle and need funds same day
Title loan borrowers at other lenders seeking to refinance at a potentially lower rate
diagnosis: Auto Money is best suited for South Carolina or Georgia residents who own a lien-free vehicle outright, have exhausted traditional credit options, and need cash on the same day without a credit check. The core caveat is cost: with monthly rates up to 25%, this is one of the most expensive forms of borrowing legally available, and borrowers who cannot repay within the 30-day term risk losing their vehicle.
services: SC Car Title Loans (up to $20,000, vehicle title as collateral)
Georgia Title Pawns (state-regulated pawn structure using vehicle title)
Motorcycle Title Loans (lien-free motorcycle titles accepted)
Same-day cash funding (under 1 hour if approved)
In-store loan servicing (cash, debit, or money order payments accepted)
meta_description: Auto Money offers vehicle title loans in South Carolina and Georgia, providing up to $20,000 same-day cash with no credit check.
